source.title: Are the Dorsal and Ventral Hippocampus Functionally Distinct Structures?

confidence_rationale: High-quality authoritative review from major labs (Fanselow: PTSD/fear conditioning; Dong: anatomical mapping). The three-compartment model (dorsal cognitive, intermediate, ventral affective) is now a standard framework. Moderate mapping fidelity: MECH-077 claims therapeutic change corresponds to remapping, and Fanselow/Dong constrains *where* that remapping must occur — ventral/intermediate HPC for emotional/trauma content.
failure_signatures: The review demonstrates a gradient rather than sharp boundary — the dorsal-ventral distinction is not binary, complicating a clean claim that MECH-077 remapping occurs exclusively in ventral HPC.
The anatomical and functional evidence is mostly from rodents; human hippocampal long-axis organization (anterior/posterior rather than ventral/dorsal) requires re-labeling.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Behavioral, anatomical, and gene expression evidence supports a tripartite segmentation of hippocampus: dorsal (cognitive, spatial navigation, precise contextual discrimination), ventral (stress, emotion, affect, motivational integration), and intermediate (transitional, both functions). Connectivity gradients reflect this: dorsal connects to sensory/motor cortex; ventral connects to amygdala, hypothalamus, and prefrontal cortex.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-077 claims therapeutic change corresponds to hippocampal remapping of harm-associated trajectories. The Fanselow/Dong framework constrains where this must occur anatomically: harm-trajectory representations and their residue field (MECH-076) are predominantly encoded in ventral (and intermediate) hippocampus, not dorsal. Therapeutic global remapping events (MECH-077) should therefore involve reorganization of ventral HPC assemblies. Dorsal HPC handles the geometric/spatial substrate; ventral handles the affective weight of trajectories.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Human hippocampal long-axis corresponds to anterior (ventral rodent function) and posterior (dorsal rodent function) — literature requires this re-labeling. The tripartite model is based largely on lesion and anatomical connectivity, not direct comparison of remapping dynamics across the axis. Whether therapeutic change actually drives hippocampal axis-specific remapping has not been tested directly.
mapping.source_context: Comprehensive review: rodent behavioral lesion studies, tract-tracing anatomical data, gene expression atlases (Allen Brain Atlas). Covers rat and mouse, with human comparisons noted.

confidence_components.notes: Authoritative review; high citation count and replicated framework. Mapping fidelity moderate — Fanselow/Dong constrains anatomy but does not directly test remapping dynamics in therapeutic or harm contexts. Transfer risk moderate due to dorsal/ventral vs. posterior/anterior re-labeling needed for human translation.
